factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities was $326 million and $370 million for the six months ended June 30, 2016 and 2015, respectively.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow is improving, showing a positive trend.

factors.summary: Grainger is experiencing price deflation which is outpacing cost deflation, indicating a loss of pricing power.

evidence.text: The gross profit margin of 40.6% during the second quarter of 2016 decreased 2.0 percentage points when compared to the same period in 2015, due primarily to price deflation exceeding cost deflation, negative customer selling mix, as well as an inventory reserves adjustment in Canada.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations – Three Months Ended June 30, 2016

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Price deflation exceeds cost deflation, indicating a significant loss of pricing power.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining in several key segments, driven by low oil prices and economic weakness.

evidence.text: Sales to natural resource customers and resellers were down in the mid-teens. Contractor customers were down in the high single digits and heavy manufacturing was down in the mid-single digits.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Declining unit volumes in key segments due to economic factors.

factors.summary: End markets such as natural resources and heavy manufacturing are slowing, while government and retail are showing slight growth.

evidence.text: Sales to natural resource customers and resellers were down in the mid-teens. Contractor customers were down in the high single digits and heavy manufacturing and commercial services were down in the mid-single digits. Low oil prices negatively impacted the performance of the heavy manufacturing and natural resources customers. These decreases were offset by mid-single digit growth in government and retail and low single digit growth in light manufacturing and transportation.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Slower end markets with only slight growth in some areas.

factors.summary: Input costs are stable or declining, but price deflation is exceeding cost deflation, negatively impacting margins.

evidence.text: The gross profit margin of 40.6% during the second quarter of 2016 decreased 2.0 percentage points when compared to the same period in 2015, due primarily to price deflation exceeding cost deflation, negative customer selling mix, as well as an inventory reserves adjustment in Canada.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations – Three Months Ended June 30, 2016

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Price deflation outpaces cost deflation, negatively affecting margins.

factors.summary: Management has revised guidance downward, indicating decreased confidence in future performance.

evidence.text: On July 19, 2016, Grainger revised the 2016 sales growth guidance from a range of 0 to 6 percent to a range of 1 to 4 percent and also revised the 2016 earnings per share guidance from a range of $11.00 to $12.80 to a range of $11.20 to $12.20. The revised guidance reflects lower than expected volume in the U.S. and Canada, partially offset by improved gross profit and operating expense leverage in the second half of the year.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Management revised guidance downward, indicating decreased confidence.

factors.summary: Management appears cautious, with lower guidance and focus on restructuring.

evidence.text: The revised guidance reflects lower than expected volume in the U.S. and Canada, partially offset by improved gross profit and operating expense leverage in the second half of the year. In addition, the Company is now expecting a higher effective tax rate for the year due to an increased concentration of earnings in higher tax rate jurisdictions and lower benefit from the clean energy investments.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Management appears cautious with lower guidance and restructuring efforts.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include weaker economic conditions, particularly in Canada, and the impact of low oil prices on key customer segments.

evidence.text: For Canada, economic growth in 2016 is forecast to continue to remain low. For the second quarter, GDP is forecast to have contracted, largely due to the negative impact of the Alberta wildfires, which are estimated by the Bank of Canada to have subtracted approximately 1 percentage point from GDP growth in the second quarter.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include weaker economic conditions and low oil prices impacting customers.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include oil prices, economic growth, and foreign exchange rates, particularly impacting Canadian operations.

evidence.text: In the United States, Business Investment and Exports are two major indicators of MRO spending. Business Investment is forecast to remain weak into 2017 primarily due to four factors: declines in oil and gas drilling, excess global capacity, a stronger U.S. dollar and slower growth in export markets. For Canada, economic growth in 2016 is forecast to continue to remain low. For the second quarter, GDP is forecast to have contracted, largely due to the negative impact of the Alberta wildfires, which are estimated by the Bank of Canada to have subtracted approximately 1 percentage point from GDP growth in the second quarter.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company faces significant risks from oil prices, economic growth, and foreign exchange rates, particularly affecting Canadian operations.

factors.summary: The acquisition of Cromwell has contributed to sales growth, but integration is ongoing.

evidence.text: Grainger completed the Cromwell Group (Holdings) Limited acquisition in the third quarter of 2015 and operating results of Cromwell have been included in the results of the Company since the acquisition date.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The acquisition of Cromwell has contributed to sales growth but integration is still ongoing with no clear positive or negative sentiment.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin segments, which has negatively impacted gross profit margins. This shift is evident in the decrease in gross profit margins over the periods mentioned, with specific mention of increased sales to lower margin customers contributing to this trend.

evidence.text: Excluding sales to Zoro, gross profit margin decreased 1.5 percentage points versus the prior year.
evidence.subsection: W.W. Grainger, Inc. and Subsidiaries MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

evidence.text: The gross profit margin during the six months ended June 30, 2016 decreased 2.6 percentage points when compared to the same period in 2015, primarily due to price deflation exceeding product cost deflation and increased sales to lower margin customers.
evidence.subsection: Matters Affecting Comparability / Results of Operations – Six Months Ended June 30, 2016

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: The shift towards lower-margin segments has negatively impacted gross profit margins significantly.

factors.summary: Overall demand is decelerating, particularly in the U.S. and Canada, with weaker economic indicators.

evidence.text: The gross profit margin for the six months ended June 30, 2016 decreased 1.8 percentage points compared to the same period in 2015, driven by unfavorable customer mix and price deflation outpacing cost deflation.
evidence.subsection: W.W. Grainger, Inc. and Subsidiaries MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

evidence.text: Grainger’s sales in the United States and Canada tend to positively correlate with Business Investment, Business Inventory, Exports and Industrial Production. In the United States, sales tend to positively correlate with Gross Domestic Product (GDP). In Canada, sales tend to positively correlate with oil prices.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand is decelerating in key markets like the U.S. and Canada, with weaker economic indicators.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating, with evidence pointing to lower-than-expected volumes in the U.S. and Canada, despite some offsetting factors such as improved gross profit and operating expense leverage.

evidence.text: Operating earnings of $681 million for the six months ended June 30, 2016 decreased 7% from $736 million for the six months ended June 30, 2015, driven by lower sales and lower gross profit margins, partially offset by expenses declining faster than sales.
evidence.subsection: W.W. Grainger, Inc. and Subsidiaries MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

evidence.text: The revised guidance reflects lower than expected volume in the U.S. and Canada, partially offset by improved gross profit and operating expense leverage in the second half of the year.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is slowing down, with lower-than-expected volumes despite some offsetting factors.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing a compression in both gross and operating margins. This is driven by factors such as price deflation outpacing cost deflation, negative customer selling mix, and higher operating expenses, particularly due to the acquisition of Cromwell.

evidence.text: The gross profit margin for the six months ended June 30, 2016 decreased 1.8 percentage points compared to the same period in 2015, driven by unfavorable customer mix and price deflation outpacing cost deflation.
evidence.subsection: W.W. Grainger, Inc. and Subsidiaries MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

evidence.text: The gross profit margin of 40.6% during the second quarter of 2016 decreased 2.0 percentage points when compared to the same period in 2015, due primarily to price deflation exceeding cost deflation, negative customer selling mix, as well as an inventory reserves adjustment in Canada. Operating expenses of $ 734 million for the second quarter of 2016 increased 2% from $717 million for the comparable 2015 quarter primarily due to the acquisition of Cromwell.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations – Three Months Ended June 30, 2016

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: The company is facing significant margin compression due to price deflation, unfavorable customer mix, and higher operating expenses.

factors.summary: Restructuring costs and efficiency programs are impacting operating expenses, with some net gains achieved through the sale of real estate, but ongoing efforts are needed to realize further savings.

evidence.text: Operating expenses in 2016 included $19 million in restructuring costs for the previously announced branch closures and the offshoring of some IT support functions. These charges were partially offset by $15 million in net gains on the sale of branch real estate for a net restructuring impact of $4 million.
evidence.subsection: W.W. Grainger, Inc. and Subsidiaries MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

evidence.text: Operating expenses in 2016 included $6 million in restructuring costs for the previously announced branch closures and the offshoring of some IT support functions. These charges were more than offset by $15 million in gains on the sale of branch real estate for a net restructuring benefit of $9 million.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring costs and efficiency programs are mixed, with some gains but ongoing efforts needed, leading to a neutral sentiment.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is increasing, which may lead to higher interest expenses. The company has various financing sources, but the rising debt levels could impact financial flexibility.

evidence.text: Grainger maintains a debt ratio and liquidity position that provide flexibility in funding working capital needs and long-term cash requirements. In addition to internally generated funds, Grainger has various sources of financing available, including bank borrowings under lines of credit. Total interest-bearing debt as a percent of total capitalization was 50.3% at June 30, 2016, and 45.8% at December 31, 2015.
evidence.subsection: W.W. Grainger, Inc. and Subsidiaries MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS / Critical Accounting Policies and Estimates

evidence.text: The increase in expense was primarily due to interest expense from the $1 billion of debt the Company issued in June 2015 and $400 million of debt issued in May 2016, as well as expected losses from the Company's investments in clean energy.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Increasing debt leverage may lead to higher interest expenses and could impact financial flexibility, posing a moderate risk.

factors.summary: The company is adjusting its inventory levels, which has resulted in a decrease in gross profit margins and increased reserves for obsolete or excess inventory. The adjustments are due to unfavorable foreign exchange and additional visibility from a SAP system conversion.

evidence.text: The gross profit margin decreased 9.4 percentage points in the six months ended June 30, 2016 versus the six months ended June 30, 2015, primarily due to an inventory adjustment in the second quarter of 2016, along with cost of goods inflation exceeding price inflation primarily due to unfavorable foreign exchange.
evidence.subsection: W.W. Grainger, Inc. and Subsidiaries MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

evidence.text: The Company maintains reserves for obsolete and excess inventory. The reserve methodology and estimates are regularly reviewed based on experience and continued demand to identify obsolete or excess quantities. During the quarter ended June 30, 2016, the Canadian business recorded an additional reserve of $10 million, as a result of additional visibility to inventory performance provided by the recent conversion to the U.S. SAP system.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company's inventory adjustments have led to decreased gross profit margins and increased reserves, indicating challenges.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are acting as a significant headwind, particularly affecting both the U.S. and Canada. In the U.S., growth is weak, and in Canada, challenges include low economic growth and negative impacts from oil prices, especially in Alberta.

evidence.text: Sales performance in Canada was primarily driven by declines within the oil and gas sector in Alberta, combined with declines in all other end markets across the country.
evidence.subsection: W.W. Grainger, Inc. and Subsidiaries MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

evidence.text: In the United States, sales tend to positively correlate with Gross Domestic Product (GDP). In Canada, sales tend to positively correlate with oil prices. The table below provides these estimated indicators for 2016: [...] For Canada, economic growth in 2016 is forecast to continue to remain low. For the second quarter, GDP is forecast to have contracted, largely due to the negative impact of the Alberta wildfires, which are estimated by the Bank of Canada to have subtracted approximately 1 percentage point from GDP growth in the second quarter.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Economic conditions, including weak growth in the U.S. and challenges in Canada due to oil prices and wildfires, are acting as significant headwinds.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange fluctuations are impacting Canadian operations, contributing to lower sales and margins.

evidence.text: The gross profit margin decreased 9.4 percentage points in the six months ended June 30, 2016 versus the six months ended June 30, 2015, primarily due to an inventory adjustment in the second quarter of 2016, along with cost of goods inflation exceeding price inflation primarily due to unfavorable foreign exchange.
evidence.subsection: W.W. Grainger, Inc. and Subsidiaries MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

evidence.text: In the three months ended June 30, 2016, eCommerce sales in Canada were $25 million, a decrease of 7% over the prior year and represented 13% of total sales. The gross profit margin decreased 11.9 percentage points in the second quarter of 2016 versus the second quarter of 2015 due largely to an inventory adjustment, along with cost of goods inflation exceeding price inflation due to unfavorable foreign exchange.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange fluctuations have negatively impacted Canadian operations, leading to lower sales and margins.
